notes_md:
**Chu choa** means “oh my!” or “wow!” — a soft exclamation for surprise, shock, or strong emotion (often positive, sometimes worried).

It’s an interjection, usually placed at the **beginning of a sentence**, often followed by a comma. It reacts to something unexpected, impressive, or sudden.

It feels expressive and a bit dramatic, similar to **“ôi”** or **“trời ơi”**, but “chu choa” can sound slightly more playful or emotional.

Use it when your reaction comes first, before the comment itself.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Oh my, I just broke the glass!
Oh my, it's raining so heavily, we have to stay here.
Oh my, this dish is so delicious, I have never tried it before.
exampleSentences: Chu choa, em vừa mới làm vỡ cái ly rồi!
Chu choa, trời mưa lớn quá, chúng ta phải ở lại đây thôi.
Chu choa, món ăn này ngon quá, anh chưa từng thử trước đây.

It feels expressive and a bit dramatic, similar to “ôi” or “trời ơi”, but “chu choa” can sound slightly more playful or emotional.
